general_info.definition: Carbon nanoparticles with an sp² hybridized carbon core used for defect passivation and photosensitization in TiO₂ composites to obtain a thin mesoporous film.

structural.crystallinity: 0.233 nm corresponding to (002) plane of amorphous carbon

applications.domain: Perovskite Solar Cells (Electron Transport Layer Modification)
applications.performance: Optimizer concentration of CnDs (3 wt%) within TiO2 resulted in a maximum power conversion efficiency(PCE) of 19.45%.16.20% higher PCE compared to pristine TiO2.
applications.comparative_advantage: CnDs composite and the defect passivation of TiO2 (achieved by carbon doping), resulted in an enhancement of current density (Jsc), open-circuit voltage (Voc) and fill factor (FF) followed by an increase in PCE. An optimized concentration of CnDs (3wt%) provided a maximum PCE of 19.45% corresponding to 11% increase in the Jsc, 70mV increase in the Voc and 10% increment in the FF when compared to the perovskite devices with pristine TiO2.
